corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

top_candidates.sentence: At training time s, the value parameters θ are updated by gradient descent on the l2 loss to the target v s , i.e., in the direction of and the policy parameters ω in the direction of the policy gradient: In order to prevent premature convergence we may add an entropy bonus, like in A3C, along the direction The overall update is obtained by summing these three gradients rescaled by appropriate coefficients, which are hyperparameters of the algorithm.
A key challenge is to handle the increased amount of data and extended training time.

We have developed a new distributed agent IMPALA (Importance Weighted Actor-Learner Architecture) that not only uses resources more efficiently in singlemachine training but also scales to thousands of machines without sacrificing data efficiency or resource utilisation.
While the improvements on tasks like the game of Go and Atari games have been dramatic, the progress has been primarily in single task performance, where an agent is trained on each task separately.
We propose the Importance Weighted Actor-Learner Architecture (IMPALA) shown in Figure .
We propose the Importance Weighted Actor-Learner Architecture (IMPALA) shown in Figure .
Therefore, we introduce the V-trace off-policy actor-critic algorithm to correct for this harmful discrepancy.
Therefore, we introduce the V-trace off-policy actor-critic algorithm to correct for this harmful discrepancy.
With the scalable architecture and V-trace combined, IM-PALA achieves exceptionally high data throughput rates of 250,000 frames per second, making it over 30 times faster than single-machine A3C.
With the scalable architecture and V-trace combined, IM-PALA achieves exceptionally high data throughput rates of 250,000 frames per second, making it over 30 times faster than single-machine A3C.

We demonstrate the effectiveness of IMPALA by training a single agent on multi-task problems using DMLab-30, a new challenge set which consists of 30 diverse cognitive tasks in the 3D DeepMind Lab environment and by training a single agent on all games in the Atari-57 set of tasks.
The most similar architecture to IMPALA is GA3C , which also uses asynchronous data collection to more effectively utilise GPUs.
The closest work to ours is the Retrace algorithm which introduced an off-policy correction for multi-step RL, and has been used in several agent architectures .
For data efficiency, computational performance and effectiveness of the off-policy correction we look at the learning behaviour of IMPALA agents trained on individual tasks.

However notice that we do not take the product of those ρ t coefficients (in contrast to the c s coefficients) so the variance does not explode with the time horizon. • c impacts the contraction modulus η of R (thus the speed at which an online-algorithm like V-trace will converge to its fixed point V πρ ).
All agents trained on Atari are equipped only with a feed forward network and pre-process frames in the same way as described in .
We have introduced a new highly scalable distributed agent, IMPALA, and a new off-policy learning algorithm, V-trace.
With its simple but scalable distributed architecture, IM-PALA can make efficient use of available compute at small and large scale.
V-trace is a general off-policy learning algorithm that is more stable and robust compared to other off-policy correction methods for actor critic agents.
We have demonstrated that IMPALA achieves better performance compared to A3C variants in terms of data efficiency, stability and final performance.
We believe that IMPALA provides a simple yet scalable and robust framework for building better Deep-RL agents and has the potential to enable research on new challenges.
This type of decoupled architecture can achieve very high throughput.
In contrast, IMPALA uses the more principled V-trace algorithm.
